title: Cooling arrangement
abstract: The invention describes a cooling arrangement (1) for a generator (2) comprising a stationary armature (20) with an interior cavity (21) bounded by an armature front plate (20F), an armature back plate (20B) and a main shaft (24), which cooling arrangement (1) comprises a partition (10) arranged to separate the interior cavity (21) into an outer annular region (21_outer) adjacent to the armature (20) and an inner annular region (21_outer) surrounding the main shaft (24), and wherein the partition (10) comprises a number of heat exchanger units (11); and a fan assembly (12) arranged in the interior cavity (21) to force hot air (AF hot ) from the outer annular region (21_outer) through the heat exchanger units (11) of the partition (10) into the inner annular region (21_outer) and to expel cooled air (AF cool) from the inner annular region (21_outer) of the interior cavity (21). The invention further comprises a wind turbine (3) comprising such a cooling arrangement (1) for cooling a generator (2); and a method of cooling a generator (2).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A cooling arrangement (1) for a generator (2) comprising a stationary armature (20) with an interior cavity (21) bounded by an armature front plate (20F) , an armature back plate (20B) and a main shaft (24), which cooling arrangement (1) comprises a partition (10) arranged to separate the interior cavity (21) into an outer annular region (21_outer) adjacent to the armature (20) and an inner annular region (21_outer) surrounding the main shaft (24), and wherein the partition (10) comprises a number of heat exchanger units (11) ; and a fan assembly (12) arranged in the interior cavity (21) to force hot air (AF hot ) from the outer annular region (21_outer) through the heat exchanger units (11) of the partition (10) into the inner annular region (21_outer) and to expel cooled air (AF C00l ) from the inner annular region (21_outer) of the interior cavity (21) . 2. A cooling arrangement according to claim 1, wherein the partition (10) extends from the armature front plate (20F) to the armature back plate (20B) . 3. A cooling arrangement according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the partition (10) is formed by an annular arrangement of a plurality of heat exchanger units (11) . 4. A cooling arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the partition (10) comprises a single annular heat exchanger unit (11) . 5. A cooling arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a heat exchanger unit (11) is part of a fluid cooling circuit (31). 6. A cooling arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a number of fans (12) arranged to expel cooled air (AF C00l ) from the interior cavity (21_inner) 

through an opening in an armature front plate (20F) and/or a number of fans (12) arranged to expel cooled air (AF C00l ) from the interior cavity (21) through an opening in an armature back plate (20B) . 7. A cooling arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a seal arrangement (100) between the partition (10) and the armature end plates (20F, 20B) . 8. A cooling arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a seal arrangement (101) between adjacent heat exchanger units (11) . 9. A wind turbine (3) with a generator (2) comprising a stationary armature (20) with an interior cavity (21) bounded by an armature front plate (20F) , an armature back plate (20B) and a main shaft (24); and further comprising a cooling arrangement (1) according to any of claims 1 to 8 for cooling the generator (2) . 10. A wind turbine according to claim 9, comprising a canopy (30) arranged to enclose the generator (2), a front end cavity (30F) between the canopy (30) and the armature front plate (20F) and/or a rear end cavity (30B) between the canopy (30) and the armature back plate (20B) , and wherein the cooling arrangement (1) is arranged to expel cooled air (AF C00l ) into the front end cavity (30F) and/or into the rear end cavity (30B) . 11. A wind turbine according to claim 9 or claim 10, comprising a fluid cooling circuit (31) connected to the heat exchanger units (11) of the cooling arrangement (1) . 12. A wind turbine according to any of claims 9 to 11, realised as a direct-drive wind turbine) , and wherein the generator (2) is realised to comprise an outer field (22) and an inner armature (20) . 13. A method of cooling a generator (2) comprising a stationary armature (20) with an interior cavity (21) bounded 

by an armature front plate (20F) , an armature back plate (20B) and a main shaft (24), which method comprises the steps of arranging a partition (10) to separate the interior cavity (21) into an outer annular region (21_outer) adjacent to the stationary armature (20) and an inner annular region (21_inner) surrounding the main shaft (24) ; arranging a number of heat exchanger units (11) in the partition (10) ; providing a fan assembly (12) in the interior cavity (21) to force hot air (AF hot ) from the outer annular region (21_outer) through the heat exchanger units (11) of the partition (10) into the inner annular region (21_inner) and to expel cooled air (AF C00l ) from the inner annular region (21_inner) of the interior cavity (21) . 14. A method according to claim 13, comprising a step of arranging a non-return valve (121) at an outlet (120) of a fan unit ( 12 ) . 15. A method according to claim 13 or claim 14, comprising a step of connecting the number of heat exchanger units (11) to a fluid cooling circuit (31) .
